title: Velvety Australian Bun Bao
description: A date-night dish featuring bun, dashi-extract, and an ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly twist.
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C. Prepare a baking sheet by lining it with parchment paper.
In a small saucepan, combine dashi-extract and shaoxing wine. Bring to a simmer over medium heat.
In a large skillet, heat vegetable oil over medium-high heat. Add ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add wild mushrooms to the skillet and cook until browned, about 4 minutes.
Add abalone to the skillet and cook for an additional 2 minutes, until browned.
Transfer the skillet contents to a baking dish. Add kombu seaweed, soy sauce, and brown sugar. Mix well.
Bake in the preheated oven for 10 minutes, or until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Meanwhile, steam the buns until heated through.
To assemble the bun bao, place a cooked bun on a plate and top with a spoonful of the abalone-mushroom mixture.
Garnish with chopped scallions and serve immediately.
